title: The Young And The Restless

jokes.text: For the last time... we are not breaking into Michael Jackson's house.
jokes.setup: Carlton describes an elaborate plan involving llamas, giraffes, monkey house, and Ferris wheel
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's talking about breaking into Michael Jackson's Neverland Ranch
jokes.explanation: Strong misdirection joke that recontextualizes Carlton's innocent tourist plan into criminal activity. Great structure with delayed punchline.

jokes.text: The reason you don't like the music is because you can't dance. Really? [Carlton attempts to dance]
jokes.setup: Will claims Carlton can't dance
jokes.punchline: Carlton proves it by attempting to dance badly
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton dancing setup - very character-specific but predictable for the series.

jokes.text: In your face.
jokes.setup: Carlton's failed dance attempt
jokes.punchline: Will's cocky response despite Carlton proving his point
jokes.explanation: Generic trash talk response, minimal craft involved.

jokes.text: Worked hard, got good grades, made the honor roll, the end.
jokes.setup: Carlton starts elaborate story about spring quarter
jokes.punchline: Reduces his entire academic achievement to a boring summary
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's earnest but dull personality.

jokes.text: Did you notice how Carlton's grown? He's almost into adult sizes.
jokes.setup: Granny commenting on Carlton's growth
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's still in children's clothing sizes
jokes.explanation: Sharp roast of Carlton's small stature delivered with grandmotherly sweetness. Good economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Are you insane? It's a little late to be asking.
jokes.setup: Philip asks if Will is insane for suggesting water park
jokes.punchline: Will points out it's too late to question his sanity now
jokes.explanation: Quick comeback that turns Philip's rhetorical question literal. Good timing.

jokes.text: Us country folks don't run to the doctor at every sneeze, sniffle, or broken bone. We are not the spendthrifts you are, Zeke.
jokes.setup: Granny lists minor ailments that don't require doctor visits
jokes.punchline: Includes 'broken bone' as if it's equally minor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that establishes Granny's tough country character while being absurd.

jokes.text: Zeke. That one always kills me.
jokes.setup: Granny calls Philip 'Zeke'
jokes.punchline: Will's appreciation for the nickname
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction that acknowledges the humor in the nickname without adding much.

jokes.text: Did you bring the picture of him and the goat? Never leave home without it.
jokes.setup: Will asks about embarrassing photo of Philip
jokes.punchline: Granny always carries it
jokes.explanation: Establishes running gag about embarrassing Philip photos that Granny keeps.

jokes.text: Just think, for the talent portion, you could twirl a checkbook.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Hilary entering a beauty contest
jokes.punchline: Her 'talent' would be showing off wealth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific roast that perfectly captures Hilary's materialistic nature. Great visual imagery.

jokes.text: They have a display on 100-year-old quilts. I got one of them in the back of my truck.
jokes.setup: Carlton suggests museum exhibit of antique quilts
jokes.punchline: Granny casually has one in her truck
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Carlton's fancy museum plans and Granny's practical country reality.

jokes.text: Did I just win the Kentucky Derby?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey puts blanket on Granny
jokes.punchline: Granny compares herself to a winning racehorse being blanketed
jokes.explanation: Clever analogy that shows Granny's wit and frustration with being pampered.

jokes.text: Why don't you just light me on fire?
jokes.setup: Philip wants to keep Granny warm
jokes.punchline: Granny suggests extreme heating method
jokes.explanation: Exaggerated complaint that shows Granny's frustration with overprotection.

jokes.text: I'm already hotter than a dog in a wool wagon.
jokes.setup: Granny complaining about being too hot
jokes.punchline: Uses colorful country metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect character voice with vivid, original country metaphor that's both funny and authentic.

jokes.text: While the gitting's good, madam.
jokes.setup: Granny tells Geoffrey to 'git'
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey plays on 'while the getting's good'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Geoffrey adapting his formal speech to Granny's dialect.

jokes.text: Now that's your first mistake. He tells me all kinds of stuff to do... and I never listen to him. And I'm a better person for it.
jokes.setup: Granny wants to follow Philip's orders
jokes.punchline: Will advises ignoring authority and claims moral superiority
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's rebellious nature and twisted logic.

jokes.text: I don't know if she'd go out with anybody in local drawers.
jokes.setup: Discussing girl who works at European underwear store
jokes.punchline: Will worries about his domestic underwear affecting his chances
jokes.explanation: Clever play on 'drawers' meaning both underwear and social class, fits Will's character.

jokes.text: That one's me, right?
jokes.setup: Granny describes big strong bull
jokes.punchline: Will immediately assumes it represents him
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's ego and need for validation, simple but character-consistent.

jokes.text: Word to Granny. That one's me, right?
jokes.setup: Granny describes handsome Jersey bull
jokes.punchline: Will immediately switches allegiance and claims this one
jokes.explanation: Good callback to previous moment, shows Will's fickle ego. 'Word to Granny' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Maybe if you changed it to cats or something.
jokes.setup: Granny's bull analogy ends with breeding season being over
jokes.punchline: Will suggests changing the animal metaphor as if that would help
jokes.explanation: Amusing literal-minded response that misses the point of the metaphor entirely.

jokes.text: I didn't know Leonard Nimoy even sang.
jokes.setup: Carlton has gift for Grandma
jokes.punchline: Reveals he got a Leonard Nimoy album, thinking it's appropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - earnest but completely tone-deaf gift choice that seems logical to him.

jokes.text: I'm picking apples from a cherry tree. What does it look like I'm doing?
jokes.setup: Philip asks what Granny is doing while she's obviously scrubbing floor
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic impossible action before stating the obvious
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback that shows Granny's feisty personality and frustration.

jokes.text: Sorry, sir, but she threatened me with a whupping.
jokes.setup: Philip questions Geoffrey about letting Granny work
jokes.punchline: Dignified butler admits fear of elderly woman's threats
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast - formal Geoffrey using informal 'whupping' and admitting intimidation.

jokes.text: The candles are starting to melt.
jokes.setup: House is too hot from Philip cranking up heat
jokes.punchline: Extreme consequence that shows how absurdly hot it is
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that escalates the overheating situation to absurd levels.

jokes.text: I am more settled than a big old box of raisin bran.
jokes.setup: Philip says Granny needs to get settled
jokes.punchline: Granny uses cereal metaphor to say she's very settled
jokes.explanation: Another great Granny metaphor that's both absurd and somehow perfectly descriptive.

jokes.text: Yo, DJ Granny.
jokes.setup: Will greeting Granny
jokes.punchline: Combines street slang 'DJ' with 'Granny'
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Will's casual familiarity and street vernacular applied to Granny.

jokes.text: Yo, DJ Granny.
jokes.setup: Will greeting Granny
jokes.punchline: Combines his street vernacular with grandmotherly title
jokes.explanation: Simple culture-mixing humor. Will's street slang applied to his grandmother shows their bonding over music.

jokes.text: Bulls and cows need to get together before it starts snowing.
jokes.setup: Will tries to use Granny's bull advice
jokes.punchline: Completely butchers the metaphor into nonsense
jokes.explanation: Great callback that shows Will completely missed the point but somehow still succeeded.

jokes.text: Who doesn't like Charlie Daniels?
jokes.setup: Will mentions Heavy D
jokes.punchline: Granny thinks of country musician Charlie Daniels instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect generational mix-up that's very true to Granny's character and era.

jokes.text: Honey, I ain't got that kind of time.
jokes.setup: Will says learning to dance takes hard study and dedication
jokes.punchline: Granny matter-of-factly references her mortality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor delivered with perfect timing and economy. Classic elderly person joke about time.

jokes.text: Granny, will you marry me? Sweet talker!
jokes.setup: Granny offers to make pancakes
jokes.punchline: Will proposes marriage, Granny playfully accepts the compliment
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing their bond, harmless family humor.

jokes.text: Please don't make me go.
jokes.setup: Will tries to get out of punishment by offering to go to Heavy D concert
jokes.punchline: Pretends concert is torture when it's what he wants
jokes.explanation: Good reverse psychology joke that's very true to Will's scheming character.

jokes.text: That boy is about as much fun as day-old oatmeal.
jokes.setup: Philip being overprotective and stern
jokes.punchline: Granny compares him to bland, unappetizing food
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Granny metaphor that vividly captures Philip's stuffiness with food imagery.

jokes.text: who has snuck out to more barn dances... hayrides, and midnight skinny-dips than a chicken has pecks.
jokes.setup: Granny revealing her rebellious past
jokes.punchline: List escalates from innocent activities to skinny-dipping, plus folksy measurement
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and character development. Shows Granny was always rebellious. 'Than a chicken has pecks' is a perfect folksy measurement.

jokes.text: who has snuck out to more barn dances... hayrides, and midnight skinny-dips than a chicken has pecks.
jokes.setup: Granny claims experience sneaking out
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly wild activities ending with skinny-dipping
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Granny's wild past, with perfect country expression at the end.

jokes.text: Good work 0070.
jokes.setup: Granny successfully deceives Philip
jokes.punchline: Will treats it like a spy mission with code number
jokes.explanation: Fun spy movie parody that treats their simple deception as high-stakes espionage.

jokes.text: Here's $10.
jokes.setup: Granny asks for code words for their escape plan
jokes.punchline: Will reveals the 'code' is just paying someone
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of spy movie tropes - the elaborate plan is just basic bribery.

jokes.text: My brother... for the last time, she is not Queen Latifah.
jokes.setup: Someone has been comparing Granny to Queen Latifah
jokes.punchline: Jazz has to repeatedly correct this comparison
jokes.explanation: Funny implication that there's been ongoing confusion about Granny's identity.

jokes.text: Word. Thank God he just had a mouthful of Gummy Bears.
jokes.setup: Philip had hideous expression on his face
jokes.punchline: It was just because he was chewing candy, not seeing them
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag explanation that provides relief after the tension of almost being caught.

jokes.text: Granny, that marriage proposal still stands.
jokes.setup: Granny pays for their food at concert
jokes.punchline: Will references earlier marriage proposal
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that reinforces their sweet relationship but adds little new.

jokes.text: With this tape, who knows how many times he could sneak out... for a whole night completely unnoticed.
jokes.setup: Carlton discovers Will's tape recorder deception
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly describes exactly what Will has done
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony where Carlton describes the exact situation without knowing it.

jokes.text: I still ain't sure how you managed to get it for $1.25 and a picture of Grandpa.
jokes.setup: T-shirt costs $10
jokes.punchline: Will somehow negotiated it down using Grandpa's photo
jokes.explanation: Great absurd detail that references earlier gag about embarrassing photos as currency.

jokes.text: bad choice, Joyce.
jokes.setup: Will suggests calling home
jokes.punchline: Granny uses outdated slang to reject the idea
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment with dated expression that fits Granny's era perfectly.

jokes.text: Ever too busy for a fan.
jokes.setup: Fan wants picture with 'Ella Fitzgerald'
jokes.punchline: Granny graciously accepts the mistaken identity
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Granny's grace and humor about being mistaken for a celebrity.

jokes.text: Call it what you like, but take it from me, Heavy D doesn't want to hear you rap.
jokes.setup: Philip claims he's not a groupie
jokes.punchline: Guard assumes Philip wants to rap for Heavy D
jokes.explanation: Funny misconception about Philip's intentions, plays on his fish-out-of-water status.

jokes.text: Call it what you like, but take it from me, Heavy D doesn't want to hear you rap.
jokes.setup: Philip claiming he's not a groupie looking for the band
jokes.punchline: Guard's assumption that Philip wants to rap for Heavy D
jokes.explanation: Good character clash with security guard making assumptions about Philip's intentions. Visual humor of Uncle Phil as wannabe rapper.

jokes.text: Nope.
jokes.setup: Philip describes Will's appearance at a rap concert
jokes.punchline: Person says no, implying Will's look is totally normal here
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that shows Philip's description fits every attendee at the concert.

jokes.text: Boo!
jokes.setup: Philip ends up on stage instead of Heavy D
jokes.punchline: Crowd boos him immediately
jokes.explanation: Classic fish-out-of-water moment that capitalizes on Philip's complete mismatch with the venue.

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, you had the shortest rap career in history.
jokes.setup: Philip's brief, disastrous stage appearance
jokes.punchline: Will frames it as a failed rap career
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up joke that reframes Philip's embarrassment as career failure.

jokes.text: What people don't pay for in Beverly Hills.
jokes.setup: Hilary mentions mud masks at fancy spa
jokes.punchline: Granny points out the absurdity of paying for dirt
jokes.explanation: Good class commentary delivered through Granny's practical country perspective.

jokes.text: That's the same look you give me at the county fair... when I wouldn't let you climb up the greasy pole.
jokes.setup: Philip giving Granny disapproving look
jokes.punchline: References specific embarrassing childhood memory
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment that reveals their history and Philip's lifelong stuffiness through specific detail.

jokes.text: Then, Zeke... let me live.
jokes.setup: Philip's overprotective behavior throughout episode
jokes.punchline: Simple but profound request that encapsulates the episode's theme
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode-ending line that's both funny and touching, with great economy and emotional weight.

tmdb_overview: Will sneaks Philip's elderly mother out of the house for a night on the town.
